ogTitle: Freemans Reach vs Freemans Waterhole: Climate Safety

heading: Freemans Reach vs Freemans Waterhole — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freemans Reach, New South Wales and Freemans Waterhole, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freemans Waterhole has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Freemans Reach (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freemans Reach or Freemans Waterhole safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freemans Reach has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Freemans Waterhole scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freemans Reach scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Freemans Waterhole scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freemans Reach or Freemans Waterhole?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freemans Waterhole has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
